document: Field-Level Data Flow Mapping — Specification Compliance Reference
spec_version: Session A v9 Architecture v4 Final (2026-03-18)

tables.description: Master word anchor. One row per English word under research. First and last table touched by every engine run.
table_spec_rules: §1.2: The registry is the anchor. Every run begins and ends with a confirmed word_registry row.
§7.1: --register inserts a new row: no=MAX(no)+1, phase1_status=Pending, automation_eligible=1. Validates: no duplicate word; lowercase and hyphens only; source_list non-empty.
§7.4: last_automation_run is the run-lock. LOCK_SENTINEL ('IN_PROGRESS') written before any API call; replaced with UTC timestamp at run end. --clear-lock sets to NULL.
§4 M03: All sequential IDs must use SELECT MAX(id)+1 queried live — never hardcoded.

fields.spec_rule: Auto-assigned sequential integer at registration. Never modified after INSERT.
fields.data_source: SELECT MAX(id) AS m FROM word_registry → (m or 0) + 1

REGISTER.value: SELECT MAX(id) AS m FROM word_registry → (m or 0) + 1

fields.spec_rule: Sequential registry number. MAX(no)+1, queried live. Used as the primary lookup key by all engine modes.
fields.data_source: SELECT MAX(no) AS m FROM word_registry → (m or 0) + 1

REGISTER.value: SELECT MAX(no) AS m FROM word_registry → (m or 0) + 1

NEW_WORD.condition: Used as WHERE key in all NEW_WORD queries

GAP_FILL.condition: Used as WHERE key per word loop

fields.spec_rule: Lowercase English word label. Validated: lowercase and hyphens only; no duplicates allowed. Never modified after INSERT.
Researcher-supplied source list label. Required non-empty at registration. Copied to wa_file_index and mti_terms at write time.

AUDIT_WORD.condition: AUDIT_WORD reads reg_row but does not modify source_list

fields.spec_rule: Optional researcher category hint. Set at registration only. No engine mode modifies it.

Pending at --register. NEW_WORD N19: 'Complete' if PASS else 'In Progress'. GAP_FILL S2: 'In Progress' at wa_file_index write; S7: final status. AUDIT_WORD A10: does NOT update phase1_status — retains current value.
fields.data_source: NEW_WORD N19/GAP_FILL S7: audit_result → 'Complete' if PASS else 'In Progress'

NEW_WORD.value: audit PASS → 'Complete'; else → 'In Progress'  |  UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_status=? WHERE no=?

GAP_FILL.value: hardcoded: 'In Progress'  |  UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_status='In Progress' ... WHERE no=?
audit PASS → retain existing; STOP/REVIEW → 'In Progress'  |  UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_status=? WHERE no=?

AUDIT_WORD.condition: A10 UPDATE does not include phase1_status — field is not set by audit mode
AUDIT_WORD.value: — GAP: spec §7.5 A10 implies status update but code omits it

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — AUDIT_WORD A10 does not update phase1_status; the field retains its pre-audit value even if audit result changes

fields.spec_rule: Set 1 at registration. NEW_WORD reads at N1 to confirm eligibility (0 → STOP). GAP_FILL S1 uses in WHERE filter. Never modified by any engine mode.

Run-lock field. LOCK_SENTINEL='IN_PROGRESS' must be written before any API call; replaced with UTC timestamp at run end. --clear-lock sets to NULL. Only NEW_WORD follows this protocol fully (N6: sentinel, N19: timestamp). GAP_FILL (S2) and AUDIT_WORD (A10) both write the completion timestamp directly without first setting the IN_PROGRESS sentinel.

NEW_WORD.condition: Before any API call — stale lock check
NEW_WORD.value: UPDATE word_registry SET last_automation_run='IN_PROGRESS' WHERE no=?
UPDATE word_registry SET last_automation_run=_now() WHERE no=?

GAP_FILL.condition: At wa_file_index write — no prior sentinel step
GAP_FILL.value: UPDATE word_registry SET ..., last_automation_run=_now() WHERE no=?
UPDATE word_registry SET last_automation_run=_now() WHERE no=?

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Always at audit end — no prior sentinel step
AUDIT_WORD.value: UPDATE word_registry SET last_automation_run=_now() WHERE no=?

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — GAP_FILL and AUDIT_WORD write the completion timestamp but neither sets LOCK_SENTINEL='IN_PROGRESS' before starting work; §7.4 run-lock guarantee requires the sentinel to be set before any API call

fields.spec_rule: FK to engine_run_log.run_id. Written at run completion by all three engine modes.

NEW_WORD.value: UPDATE word_registry SET automation_run_id=run_id WHERE no=?

GAP_FILL.value: UPDATE word_registry SET automation_run_id=run_id WHERE no=?
UPDATE word_registry SET automation_run_id=run_id WHERE no=?

AUDIT_WORD.value: UPDATE word_registry SET automation_run_id=run_id WHERE no=?

fields.spec_rule: Count of wa_term_inventory rows for this word. Written at run completion by all three engine modes.
fields.data_source: N19: counts['total_terms_new'] from run loop; GAP_FILL S4/S7 and AUDIT_WORD A10: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_term_inventory WHERE file_id IN (...)

GAP_FILL.value: UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_term_count=SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_term_inventory WHERE file_id=? WHERE no=?
UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_term_count=term_count WHERE no=?

AUDIT_WORD.value: UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_term_count=SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_term_inventory WHERE file_id IN (...) WHERE no=?

fields.spec_rule: Count of span-confirmed verse records (span_strong_match=1 OR IS NULL). Written at run completion by all three engine modes.
fields.data_source: N19: counts['total_verses_inserted']; GAP_FILL S4/S7 and AUDIT_WORD A10: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_verse_records WHERE file_id IN (...) AND (span_strong_match=1 OR IS NULL)

GAP_FILL.value: UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_verse_count=SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_verse_records WHERE file_id=? AND (span_strong_match=1 OR IS NULL) WHERE no=?
UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_verse_count=verse_count WHERE no=?

AUDIT_WORD.value: UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_verse_count=SELECT COUNT(*) FROM wa_verse_records WHERE file_id IN (...) AND (span_strong_match=1 OR IS NULL) WHERE no=?

fields.spec_rule: Stores the run_id of the NEW_WORD run that created the word's data. Written only by NEW_WORD N19. GAP_FILL and AUDIT_WORD do not write this field.

NEW_WORD.value: UPDATE word_registry SET phase1_input_file=run_id WHERE no=?

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — GAP_FILL and AUDIT_WORD do not write phase1_input_file; field remains NULL or retains NEW_WORD value; spec §9 implies it should record the originating run for any mode that creates the word's Phase 1 data

fields.spec_rule: No spec rule — field exists in DB schema and engine code (GAP_FILL S1 UPDATE) but is not referenced anywhere in the v9 Architecture specification.
fields.data_source: STEP REST API text search via StepClient.get_strongs_for_word(word) → JSON array e.g. [{"strong":"H7965","count":148},...]

GAP_FILL.value: UPDATE word_registry SET strongs_list=json_array WHERE no=?

fields.spec_rule: AUDIT_WORD A10 is specified to update last_automation_run, automation_run_id, and notes. Researcher annotation field. However, A10 UPDATE in code only sets phase1_term_count, phase1_verse_count, last_automation_run, automation_run_id — notes is absent.
fields.data_source: Not implemented — A10 UPDATE in audit_word.py does not include notes column

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Spec says update — code does not implement it
AUDIT_WORD.value: — DIVERGES: UPDATE word_registry SET notes=? WHERE no=? is absent from A10

fields.spec_match: ✗ Diverges — spec §7.5 A10 lists notes in the update statement but audit_word.py A10 UPDATE does not include the notes column; field is never written by any engine mode

tables.description: One row per word. Anchor connecting word_registry to all term and verse data. Created by NEW_WORD N9 or GAP_FILL S2. AUDIT_WORD reads it at A1 and updates testament_coverage at A9; never re-creates it.
table_spec_rules: §7.2 N9: INSERT one row inside atomic transaction N9-N15. id=MAX(id)+1. testament_coverage=NULL at insert; derived at N14.

§7.3: part_number, total_parts, is_split, root_families_in_prior_parts are legacy columns from the manual multi-part pipeline. v9 always writes NULL/0 for these. Retained in schema for backward compatibility.
§9: specification constant = 'Session A v9 Automation'. phase = 'Phase 1'. translation_used = 'ESV'. All hardcoded.
§4 M03: id must be queried live via SELECT MAX(id)+1 — never hardcoded.
§7.5 A9: AUDIT_WORD re-derives testament_coverage from confirmed verse rows and updates wa_file_index.

fields.spec_rule: Sequential integer. Must query MAX(id) live before insert.
fields.data_source: engine/db.py get_max_id(conn, 'wa_file_index') → SELECT MAX(id) FROM wa_file_index → result + 1

GAP_FILL.condition: Phase A, per word — idempotent; skipped if already exists

AUDIT_WORD.condition: SELECT id FROM wa_file_index WHERE registry_id=? — no row → STOP

fields.spec_rule: Stores run_id — not a filesystem path. v9 replaced the prior .md filename convention with run_id.
word_registry.no value stored as TEXT. Links file_index row back to registry.
word_registry.id (PK integer, not .no). FK constraint to word_registry.
fields.data_source: SELECT * FROM word_registry WHERE no=? → reg_row['id']

fields.spec_rule: English word label copied from word_registry.word.
fields.data_source: SELECT word FROM word_registry WHERE no=? → reg_row['word']

fields.spec_rule: Legacy column from v3-v8 manual multi-part pipeline. v9 always writes NULL. Column retained for backward compatibility.
Legacy column. v9 always writes NULL.
Legacy column. v9 always writes 0.
Constant string identifying the automation spec version. Written from engine/constants.py.
fields.data_source: engine/constants.py → SPECIFICATION constant = 'Session A v9 Automation'

fields.spec_rule: Constant 'Phase 1'. Hardcoded for all v9 engine runs.
UTC date of the run that created this row. YYYY-MM-DD format.
Constant 'ESV'. Hardcoded for all v9 engine runs.
Copied from word_registry.source_list at file_index creation.
fields.data_source: SELECT source_list FROM word_registry WHERE no=? → reg_row['source_list']

fields.spec_rule: NULL at INSERT. Derived after verse records are committed by querying distinct testament values from confirmed verse rows (span_strong_match=1). Re-derived in AUDIT_WORD A9 from span_strong_match=1 OR IS NULL rows.
fields.data_source: SELECT DISTINCT testament FROM wa_verse_records WHERE file_id=? AND span_strong_match=1 → 'OT_only' / 'NT_only' / 'both' / NULL

NEW_WORD.value: NULL — placeholder; derived post-transaction at N14
SELECT DISTINCT testament FROM wa_verse_records WHERE file_id=? AND span_strong_match=1 → UPDATE wa_file_index SET testament_coverage=? WHERE id=?

GAP_FILL.value: NULL — placeholder; no derivation step in GAP_FILL

GAP_FILL.condition: GAP_FILL has no testament_coverage derivation step after S3 verse insert — field remains NULL
GAP_FILL.value: — GAP: no equivalent of N14 in GAP_FILL stream

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Always — re-derives from confirmed verses post-audit
AUDIT_WORD.value: SELECT DISTINCT testament FROM wa_verse_records WHERE file_id=? AND (span_strong_match=1 OR IS NULL) → UPDATE wa_file_index SET testament_coverage=? WHERE id=?

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — GAP_FILL inserts the row with testament_coverage=NULL at S2 but has no derivation step; the field is never updated after verses are written by GAP_FILL (S3). AUDIT_WORD A9 corrects this for words it processes.

fields.spec_rule: Legacy multi-part column. Never written by v9 engine. Retained in schema for backward compatibility only.

tables.description: Master term index. One row per Strong's number processed. Created by NEW_WORD N10 or GAP_FILL S2 Phase C. Idempotent — re-checks existence before INSERT. AUDIT_WORD spec intent: INSERT any term returned by STEP API that is missing from mti_terms; flag as redundant any row in mti_terms not returned by STEP. Code currently does not implement either action.
table_spec_rules: §7.2 N10: INSERT per NEW term inside atomic transaction N9-N15.
§9: status hardcoded 'extracted'. extraction_date = run date. strongs_reconciled = 1 if suffix was resolved (input != resolved), else 0.
§4 M03: id must be queried live via SELECT MAX(id)+1.
owning_part is a legacy multi-part column — always NULL in v9.
Idempotent: both NEW_WORD and GAP_FILL re-check SELECT id FROM mti_terms WHERE strongs_number=? before INSERT.

AUDIT_WORD spec intent (not yet implemented): (1) For each strongs_number returned by STEP API for this word — if not present in mti_terms, INSERT the row (same columns as NEW_WORD N10). (2) For each row already in mti_terms for this word — if its strongs_number is NOT in the STEP API response, UPDATE status='REDUNDANT'.

fields.spec_rule: Sequential integer. Must query MAX(id) live before insert.
fields.data_source: engine/db.py get_max_id(conn, 'mti_terms') → SELECT MAX(id) FROM mti_terms → result + 1

NEW_WORD.condition: Part of atomic transaction N9-N15; only if strongs_number not already in mti_terms

GAP_FILL.condition: Idempotent: only if SELECT id FROM mti_terms WHERE strongs_number=? returns no row

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Loads existing mti_terms rows for this word
Should INSERT for each strongs_number in STEP response not already in mti_terms — not yet implemented
AUDIT_WORD.value: — DIVERGES: code never calls get_max_id(conn, 'mti_terms') or INSERT in audit_word.py

fields.spec_match: ✗ Diverges — AUDIT_WORD spec intent requires INSERT of missing term rows (terms in STEP API but absent from mti_terms); code does not implement this; all mti_terms fields are unset by AUDIT_WORD

fields.spec_rule: Resolved Strong's number (e.g. H7965). If input had a suffix that was resolved, stores the resolved form.
Transliteration of the Strong's term. From STEP vocab API response.
Primary gloss for the term. From STEP vocab API response.
Derived from STEP vocab API 'language' field. 'Hebrew' or 'Greek'.
word_registry.no stored as string. Links this term to its registry entry.
English word label from word_registry. Denormalised copy for query convenience.
fields.data_source: SELECT word FROM word_registry WHERE no=? → reg_row['word']

fields.spec_rule: Legacy multi-part column. v9 always writes NULL.
Stores wa_file_index.id as a string. Links mti_terms row to its file index entry.
fields.data_source: str(file_id) — wa_file_index.id just inserted at N9/S2

NEW_WORD.condition: Part of transaction; file_id available from N9

GAP_FILL.condition: Idempotent INSERT; file_id from Phase A

fields.spec_rule: 'extracted' at INSERT (hardcoded). AUDIT_WORD spec intent: UPDATE status='REDUNDANT' for any row whose strongs_number is not returned by the STEP API for this word.
fields.data_source: INSERT: hardcoded 'extracted' | AUDIT_WORD UPDATE: 'REDUNDANT' (confirmed flag value)

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Should UPDATE status='REDUNDANT' for any row whose strongs_number is absent from STEP API response — not yet implemented
AUDIT_WORD.value: — DIVERGES: UPDATE mti_terms SET status='REDUNDANT' WHERE id=? not present in audit_word.py

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — INSERT correctly sets 'extracted'; AUDIT_WORD should UPDATE status='REDUNDANT' for stale term rows (strongs_number absent from STEP API response) but code does not implement it

fields.spec_rule: UTC date of the run that created this term row. YYYY-MM-DD format.
1 if the resolved strongs_number differs from the input (suffix stripped/mapped); 0 otherwise.

tables.description: One row per Strong's number per word. Holds all per-term metadata: glosses, meaning, occurrence counts, testament coverage, parsed meaning FK. Written at N11 (NEW_WORD) and S2 Phase C (GAP_FILL). Refreshed non-destructively at A4 (AUDIT_WORD).
table_spec_rules: §7.2 N11: INSERT per NEW term inside atomic transaction N9–N15. id=MAX(id)+1 queried live.
§9: god_as_subject=0, somatic_link=0 — DEFERRED. Next-phase judgment fields.
§9: also_spelled and occurrence_count_qualifier — DEFERRED-INTERACTIVE. Filled at field-fill N18/A8/S8. null until researcher provides value or confirms null.
§9: testament — derived from confirmed verse records after verse insert (span_strong_match=1). Derived per term at N14. GAP_FILL and AUDIT_WORD do not re-derive this field after verse changes.
§4 M04: short_def_mounce and parsed_meaning_id are new columns added in migration M04.
AUDIT_WORD A4: non-destructive COALESCE update — fills only null/empty fields. Never overwrites existing values.
GAP_FILL S4: meaning gap-fill — fills meaning, meaning_numbered, step_search_gloss, word_analysis_gloss, lsj_entry if null. Also fills via COALESCE.
WR-11: transliteration must be non-null — STOP condition.
WR-12: language must be 'Hebrew' or 'Greek' — STOP condition.

fields.spec_rule: Sequential integer. Must query MAX(id) live before insert.
fields.data_source: engine/db.py get_max_id(conn, 'wa_term_inventory') → SELECT MAX(id) FROM wa_term_inventory → result + 1

NEW_WORD.condition: Part of atomic transaction N9–N15; one row per NEW term

GAP_FILL.condition: One row per NEW term; idempotent within transaction

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Refreshes existing rows; never INSERTs new rows (new terms are handled at A6 gap-fill)
AUDIT_WORD.value: COALESCE update — no new id assigned

fields.spec_rule: FK to wa_file_index.id. Links this term row to the file anchor for this word.
fields.data_source: wa_file_index.id assigned at N9/S2 Phase A

NEW_WORD.condition: Part of transaction; file_id from N9

fields.spec_rule: H-prefix Strong's → 'Hebrew'; G-prefix → 'Greek'. WR-12 STOP if not one of these two values.
STEP internal code — stores resolved Strong's number. Same as strongs_number at write time.
Canonical resolved Strong's number. H2428 → H2428A. Same as term_id at write time.
Required — WR-11 STOP if null. From STEP vocab API.
English gloss label from STEP search heading (vocabInfos[0].stepGloss). COALESCE-filled in S4 and A4 — never overwrites existing non-empty value.

AUDIT_WORD.condition: COALESCE(NULLIF(step_search_gloss,''),?) — fills only if null or empty string

fields.spec_rule: Gloss from STEP word analysis block. Same as step_search_gloss at write time. Researcher may update in review. COALESCE-filled in S4 and A4.
Token occurrence count from STEP API. Null if API does not return it — triggers DATA_GAP quality flag. COALESCE-filled by AUDIT_WORD A4.
fields.data_source: vocab.get('occurrence_count') from STEP API — may be null

NEW_WORD.condition: Part of transaction; null if API returns no value

AUDIT_WORD.condition: COALESCE(occurrence_count, ?) — fills only if currently null

fields.spec_rule: DEFERRED-INTERACTIVE. 'about', 'exactly', etc. Not available from API. Filled at field-fill N18/A8/S8 with researcher input or confirmed null. Only NEW_WORD N18 implements the interactive field-fill; GAP_FILL has no S8 equivalent in code; AUDIT_WORD A8 in code writes SPAN_BACK_POPULATED flag (not field-fill).
fields.data_source: Researcher input at N18 field-fill — not from API

NEW_WORD.condition: Interactive: presented only if null; researcher types value or presses Enter to confirm null
NEW_WORD.value: UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET occurrence_count_qualifier=? WHERE id=?

GAP_FILL.condition: GAP_FILL has no S8 field-fill stream implemented; field remains null for words processed by BULK_GAP_FILL without a subsequent NEW_WORD run

AUDIT_WORD.condition: A8 in code writes SPAN_BACK_POPULATED quality flag — not field-fill; occurrence_count_qualifier not updated by AUDIT_WORD

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — only NEW_WORD N18 implements the interactive field-fill; GAP_FILL has no S8 equivalent in code; AUDIT_WORD A8 writes SPAN_BACK_POPULATED flag instead of field-fill; words processed only through GAP_FILL will have occurrence_count_qualifier=null indefinitely

fields.spec_rule: Full meaning block from STEP vocab.medium_def. HTML-stripped; newlines preserved. Null if API returns none — triggers NO_WORD_ANALYSIS flag. Gap-filled in S4; refreshed in A4.

GAP_FILL.condition: Fills if meaning IS NULL or empty
GAP_FILL.value: UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET meaning=?,meaning_numbered=? WHERE id=?

AUDIT_WORD.value: UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET meaning=COALESCE(NULLIF(meaning,''),?) WHERE id=?

fields.spec_rule: Same content as meaning at write time. Mirrors meaning text. Researcher-distinguishable numbered form for future structured parsing. Gap-filled and refreshed same as meaning.
fields.data_source: vocab.get('medium_def') — same value as meaning at write

GAP_FILL.value: UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET meaning_numbered=? WHERE id=?

fields.spec_rule: DEFERRED-INTERACTIVE. Hebrew only. Alternate spelling / Strong's variant (JSON). Filled at field-fill N18/A8/S8. null until researcher provides value or confirms null. Same gap as occurrence_count_qualifier — only NEW_WORD N18 implements field-fill.
fields.data_source: Researcher input at N18 field-fill — Hebrew terms only; not from API

NEW_WORD.condition: Hebrew terms only; presented if null; researcher types value or confirms null
NEW_WORD.value: UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET also_spelled=? WHERE id=?

GAP_FILL.condition: No S8 field-fill in GAP_FILL code

AUDIT_WORD.condition: A8 in code writes quality flag, not field-fill

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — only NEW_WORD N18 implements the field-fill; GAP_FILL and AUDIT_WORD do not prompt for also_spelled; Hebrew words processed only through GAP_FILL will have also_spelled=null indefinitely

fields.spec_rule: Greek only. Full LSJ lexicon text from STEP vocab API. Null for Hebrew. COALESCE-filled by S4 and A4.
fields.data_source: vocab.get('lsj_entry') from STEP API — null for Hebrew

NEW_WORD.condition: Part of transaction; null for Hebrew

GAP_FILL.value: UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET lsj_entry=COALESCE(lsj_entry,?) WHERE id=?

AUDIT_WORD.condition: COALESCE(NULLIF(lsj_entry,''),?) — Greek only; null for Hebrew

fields.spec_rule: Greek only. Mounce short definition. New column added in M04. Null for Hebrew. COALESCE-filled by A4.
fields.data_source: vocab.get('short_def_mounce') from STEP API — null for Hebrew

NEW_WORD.condition: Part of transaction; null for Hebrew

fields.spec_rule: OT/NT/both derived per term from confirmed verse records (span_strong_match=1). Derived at NEW_WORD N14 post-transaction. GAP_FILL inserts verses at S3 but has no per-term testament derivation step. AUDIT_WORD A9 updates wa_file_index.testament_coverage only — not wa_term_inventory.testament.
fields.data_source: SELECT DISTINCT testament FROM wa_verse_records WHERE term_inv_id=? AND span_strong_match=1

NEW_WORD.value: NULL — placeholder; derived post-transaction at N14
SELECT DISTINCT testament FROM wa_verse_records WHERE term_inv_id=? AND span_strong_match=1 → OT/NT/both → UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET testament=? WHERE id=?

GAP_FILL.condition: S3 inserts verse records but no step derives per-term testament for wa_term_inventory in GAP_FILL
GAP_FILL.value: — GAP: field remains NULL after GAP_FILL verse insert (S3)

AUDIT_WORD.condition: A9 updates wa_file_index.testament_coverage only; wa_term_inventory.testament is not updated
AUDIT_WORD.value: — GAP: per-term testament not re-derived after A3a verse sync

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — only NEW_WORD N14 derives per-term testament from confirmed verse records; GAP_FILL (S3) and AUDIT_WORD (A3a/A9) do not update wa_term_inventory.testament after verse changes

fields.spec_rule: DEFERRED. Default 0 (schema DEFAULT). Next-phase judgment — whether the term is applied to God or Holy Spirit. Never set by engine; researcher assigns in next phase.
fields.data_source: Schema DEFAULT 0 — not written by engine INSERT column list

NEW_WORD.condition: Not in INSERT column list; schema DEFAULT 0 applies

GAP_FILL.condition: Not in column list; schema DEFAULT 0

fields.spec_rule: DEFERRED. Default 0 (schema DEFAULT). Inner state linked to bodily expression — next-phase judgment. Never set by engine.
fields.data_source: Schema DEFAULT 0 — not written by engine INSERT column list

NEW_WORD.condition: Not in INSERT column list; schema DEFAULT 0 applies

fields.spec_rule: 1 if medium_def contains Hiphil or Piel (Hebrew) or causative sense. Derived at INSERT from vocab API response.
step_client warnings for this term. Null if no warnings. Populated at INSERT from any errors accumulated during pre-transaction fetch.

NEW_WORD.condition: Part of transaction; null if no errors

GAP_FILL.condition: Always NULL in GAP_FILL Phase C

fields.spec_rule: FK to wa_meaning_parsed.id. Null until meaning parser runs. Set by run_parser_for_file() at N15 (NEW_WORD), S4 (GAP_FILL), A5 (AUDIT_WORD).
fields.data_source: engine/meaning_parser.py run_parser_for_file() → INSERT wa_meaning_parsed → UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET parsed_meaning_id=?

NEW_WORD.value: run_parser_for_file() → UPDATE wa_term_inventory SET parsed_meaning_id=wa_meaning_parsed.id WHERE id=?

tables.description: Related words cluster for each term from the STEP word analysis block. One row per related word per term. Written at N11 (NEW_WORD) and S2 Phase C (GAP_FILL). AUDIT_WORD does not refresh or add related words — if the STEP API returns new related words in a re-fetch, they are not captured.
table_spec_rules: §7.2 N11: INSERT per related word per NEW term inside atomic transaction N9–N15.
Related words are written inside the same transaction as wa_term_inventory (Phase C in GAP_FILL).
AUDIT_WORD A4 re-fetches vocab and does COALESCE UPDATEs on wa_term_inventory but does not INSERT new wa_term_related_words rows. If the STEP API returns new related words, they are silently dropped.
relationship_note column exists in schema but is not written by any engine mode — researcher annotation field.

fields.spec_rule: Sequential integer. AUTOINCREMENT in schema — no explicit MAX query required.
fields.data_source: SQLite AUTOINCREMENT — engine does not explicitly set id for related_words rows

NEW_WORD.condition: One row per related word per NEW term
NEW_WORD.value: AUTOINCREMENT — not explicitly set in INSERT

GAP_FILL.condition: One row per related word per NEW term

AUDIT_WORD.condition: A4 re-fetches vocab but does not INSERT new related_words rows; new related words from STEP API are not captured
AUDIT_WORD.value: — GAP: AUDIT_WORD silently drops new related words from re-fetch

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — AUDIT_WORD does not INSERT new related word rows even when re-fetching vocab at A4; related words added to STEP API after the initial write are never captured

fields.spec_rule: FK to wa_term_inventory.id. Links related word row to its owning term.
fields.data_source: ti_id — wa_term_inventory.id from concurrent N11/S2-Phase-C INSERT

fields.spec_rule: English gloss for the related word, from STEP vocab related_words array.
Transliteration of the related word. From STEP vocab related_words array.
Strong's number of the related word. From STEP vocab related_words array.
Schema column for researcher annotation of how related words relate to the term. Never written by any engine mode. Researcher-only field.

tables.description: Core research table. One row per confirmed term occurrence in a verse (span_strong_match=1 at write time). All v9 engine modes write or modify this table. NEW_WORD N12 and GAP_FILL S3 INSERT span-confirmed rows only. AUDIT_WORD A3a INSERTs missing rows, UPDATEs existing rows with latest STEP data, and marks orphan rows (span_strong_match=-1) for verses in DB but absent from current STEP API response.
table_spec_rules: §5.2: Span filter — only verses where queried Strong's appears in the strong= attribute of the target-word span are stored. span_strong_match=1 at write time for all v9 INSERTs.

§5.5 A3a: AUDIT_WORD verse sync — INSERT missing (in STEP not in DB), UPDATE existing (overwrite span fields), mark orphans span_strong_match=-1 (in DB not in STEP). -1 is a code extension not in the spec's defined value set (spec: 1/0/NULL only).
§4 M05: target_word, span_strong_match, context_before, context_after added in migration M05.
§5.6: context_before, context_after, note, claude_output — Phase 2 reserved. Always NULL in Phase 1. Not written by v9 engine.
§9: translation hardcoded 'ESV' in all INSERT statements.
§9: book_id derived from book_code via book_code_variants lookup — WR error if not found.

fields.spec_rule: Sequential integer. Must query MAX(id) live before insert.

NEW_WORD.condition: One row per span-confirmed verse per term

GAP_FILL.condition: Terms with zero verse records; span-confirmed only

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Verses in STEP response but absent from DB

fields.spec_rule: FK to wa_file_index.id.
fields.data_source: file_id from wa_file_index INSERT at N9/S2 Phase A

fields.spec_rule: FK to wa_term_inventory.id.
fields.data_source: ti_id from wa_term_inventory INSERT at N11/S2 Phase C

fields.spec_rule: Strong's / STEP code for the term this verse belongs to.
Transliteration of the term. From vocab at write time.
FK to books.id. Derived from book_code via SELECT book_id FROM book_code_variants WHERE code=?. STOP if not found.
fields.data_source: engine/db.py get_book_id(conn, rec['book_code']) → SELECT book_id FROM book_code_variants WHERE code=?

fields.spec_rule: STEP verse reference string as returned, e.g. 'Gen 31:27'.

AUDIT_WORD.value: rec['ref'] — used as match key for INSERT vs UPDATE decision

fields.spec_rule: Integer chapter number. Single-chapter books always 1. Parsed from STEP verse record.
Integer verse number. Parsed from STEP verse record.
'OT' or 'NT' — derived from book_code vs NT_BOOKS list in step_client.
fields.data_source: rec['testament'] from STEP verse record (derived by step_client)

fields.spec_rule: Hardcoded 'ESV' for all v9 engine runs.
HTML-stripped ESV verse text from STEP masterSearch preview.
fields.data_source: rec['esv_text'] from STEP masterSearch; HTML-stripped by step_client

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Existing verse: always overwritten with latest STEP text
AUDIT_WORD.value: UPDATE wa_verse_records SET verse_text=? WHERE id=?

fields.spec_rule: English rendering of target Strong's from span text. Null if span extraction fails. Set at INSERT for all v9 writes. AUDIT_WORD A3a also UPDATEs if new value available from re-fetch.
fields.data_source: rec.get('target_word', '') — extracted by span_filter.py from strong= attribute span text

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Existing verse: if rec.get('target_word') is truthy
AUDIT_WORD.value: UPDATE wa_verse_records SET target_word=? WHERE id=?

fields.spec_rule: 1 = queried Strong's confirmed in span. 0 = only sibling/prefix form (AUDIT back-pop). NULL = pre-v9 not yet assessed. Code also uses -1 for orphan rows (in DB but not in current STEP API response) — this value is not in the spec's defined set.
fields.data_source: rec.get('span_strong_match', 1) at INSERT; span_filter.py derived; AUDIT A3a: 1 or -1 on UPDATE

NEW_WORD.condition: Span-confirmed rows only — all inserted rows have span_strong_match=1

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Orphan: verse in DB but absent from STEP API response
AUDIT_WORD.value: UPDATE wa_verse_records SET span_strong_match=-1 WHERE id=?  — NOTE: -1 is code-only; spec defines 1/0/NULL

fields.spec_match: ⚠ Partial — INSERT correctly sets span_strong_match=1 per spec §5.2; AUDIT_WORD A3a uses span_strong_match=-1 for orphan rows (verses in DB but absent from STEP response); spec §5.2/§5.5 only defines 1 (confirmed), 0 (sibling/back-pop), NULL (pre-v9 unassessed); -1 is a code extension

fields.spec_rule: Phase 2 reserved. Always NULL in Phase 1. AUDIT_WORD A3a may UPDATE if STEP API returns a value (conditional write), but STEP never returns this — effectively always NULL.
fields.data_source: NULL — Phase 2 reserved. Not written in Phase 1.

NEW_WORD.value: NULL — Phase 2 reserved; hardcoded in INSERT

GAP_FILL.value: NULL — Phase 2 reserved; hardcoded in INSERT

AUDIT_WORD.condition: Missing verse INSERT: NULL; UPDATE: conditional on rec.get('context_before') is not None — never triggered in Phase 1
AUDIT_WORD.value: NULL for Phase 1 — STEP API does not return context_before

fields.spec_rule: Phase 2 reserved. Always NULL in Phase 1. Same logic as context_before.
fields.data_source: NULL — Phase 2 reserved. Not written in Phase 1.

AUDIT_WORD.condition: NULL; conditional UPDATE never triggered in Phase 1

fields.spec_rule: Phase 2 reserved. Researcher annotation at verse level. Never written by v9 engine. NULL for all Phase 1 records.
Phase 2 reserved. AI-assisted analysis output for specific verse. Never written by v9 engine. NULL for all Phase 1 records.

tables.description: Root family labels assigned to each term from wa_term_inventory. One row per root family membership per term — a term may belong to multiple families. Entirely researcher-assigned. The engine never writes to this table. All rows null for automation terms (v9). WR-13 explicitly excludes root_code from the undocumented-null check.
table_spec_rules: §9: wa_term_root_family.root_code — Researcher judgment | null until assigned | DEFERRED. Not written by engine. Expected null for all automation terms. Not flagged by WR-13.
§14: Content | Researcher (deferred) | Unchanged — populated by researcher, not engine.
§8.1 WR-13: wa_term_root_family.root_code is explicitly excluded from the undocumented-null quality flag check.
No engine mode (NEW_WORD, GAP_FILL, AUDIT_WORD) writes to this table. Rows appear only when a researcher assigns root families to a term (next-phase work).
~640 rows in DB baseline — all written by researcher during earlier manual processing phases.

fields.spec_rule: AUTOINCREMENT PK. Not assigned by engine — researcher writes these rows.
FK to wa_term_inventory.id. Links root family row to its owning term. Assigned by researcher.
Root family identifier, e.g. 'TSR', 'CHARAH', 'LUPE'. Researcher judgment — null until assigned. DEFERRED. WR-13 explicitly excludes from undocumented-null check.
'Hebrew', 'Greek', or 'Aramaic'. Researcher-assigned. Not written by engine.
Brief English gloss for the root, e.g. 'to be angry', 'inner peace'. Researcher-assigned. Not written by engine.
Additional researcher note about this root family membership. Not written by engine.

tables.description: Lookup table for Phase 2 analytical flags. 25 rows. Seeded historically by migration scripts and patch imports — not written by any run mode. Both wa_term_phase2_flags and mti_term_flags FK to this single table. All 25 flags are researcher judgment flags; none are written by the v9 engine. Spec §14 classifies this table as Reference with no mode.
table_spec_rules: §14: Reference | — | Unchanged. No run mode writes to this table.
25 rows populated via historical migration (migrate_flag_tables.py consolidated wa_phase2_flag_types into this table at normalisation).

All 25 phase2_flag_types entries represent researcher judgment flags — none are derivable by the engine. Spec §13 (checklist): 'Judgment-deferred fields confirmed: wa_term_root_family, god_as_subject, somatic_link, mti_term_flags, all 25 phase2_flag_types judgment flags.'
flag_engine.py does not reference this table when writing flags — only wa_quality_flag_types is used for derivable flags.

fields.spec_rule: AUTOINCREMENT PK. Seeded by migration; read only at runtime.
fields.data_source: Seeded by migration — read only by run modes
fields.spec_match: — Reference table (seeded by migration; no run mode writes)

fields.spec_rule: 25 analytical flag codes, e.g. 'GOD_AS_SUBJECT', 'SOMATIC_INNER_LINK', 'THEOLOGICAL_ANCHOR'. UNIQUE constraint. Seeded by migration.
fields.data_source: Seeded by migration — read only at runtime. Run modes FK-reference via wa_term_phase2_flags.flag_id.
fields.spec_match: — Reference table (seeded by migration; no run mode writes)

fields.spec_rule: Human-readable description of the flag. Seeded by migration.
fields.data_source: Seeded by migration — read only at runtime
fields.spec_match: — Reference table (seeded by migration; no run mode writes)

tables.description: Junction table: wa_term_inventory ↔ phase2_flag_types (many-to-many). Composite PK (term_inv_id, flag_id) — no separate id column. Spec says engine writes 'derivable flags' here at NEW_WORD N16 and GAP_FILL S5. However, flag_engine.py's run_flag_engine() writes ONLY to wa_data_quality_flags and never INSERTs to this table. No INSERT INTO wa_term_phase2_flags exists anywhere in engine/ code. In practice, all rows written to this table come from manual DB patch imports, not from engine runs.
table_spec_rules: §7.2 N16: 'Run flag engine. Evaluate derivable flags. INSERT wa_term_phase2_flags, wa_data_quality_flags (including SPAN_RESOLUTION_CONFLICT for any span-conflict terms).'
§6.1 S5: 'FLAG_ENGINE | Terms written in S4 | wa_term_phase2_flags (derivable flags); wa_data_quality_flags including SPAN_RESOLUTION_CONFLICT where applicable.'
§14: 'Content | Engine (derivable) | Unchanged' — spec intent is engine-written.

CODE REALITY: flag_engine.py run_flag_engine() docstring says 'Writes rows to wa_term_phase2_flags and wa_data_quality_flags' but implementation only writes to wa_data_quality_flags. No INSERT INTO wa_term_phase2_flags anywhere in engine/ code.

CODE REALITY: flag_engine.py comment: 'All other phase2_flag_types are judgment flags deferred to the researcher.' Derivable quality flags go to wa_data_quality_flags (HIGH_FREQUENCY_ANCHOR, THIN_DATA, SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE, NO_WORD_ANALYSIS, NO_VERSES, SPAN_RESOLUTION_CONFLICT).
AUDIT_WORD has no explicit flag engine step in its step sequence — wa_term_phase2_flags is not touched.
Impact: Terms processed by v9 automation engine have zero rows in wa_term_phase2_flags. The table has rows only for terms processed before v9 (manual patches).
COMPOSITE PK — no separate id column. Fields are term_inv_id and flag_id only.

fields.spec_rule: FK to wa_term_inventory.id. Part of composite PK. Spec: written by engine at N16 (NEW_WORD) and S5 (GAP_FILL). Code: never set — run_flag_engine() does not INSERT to this table.
fields.data_source: NOT_WRITTEN by engine — rows written only via manual DB patch imports
fields.py_file: engine/flag_engine.py (listed in docstring but not implemented)

NEW_WORD.condition: Spec says 'INSERT wa_term_phase2_flags (derivable flags)' at N16; code run_flag_engine() never INSERTs to this table

GAP_FILL.condition: Spec says FLAG_ENGINE writes wa_term_phase2_flags at S5; code run_flag_engine() never INSERTs to this table

fields.spec_match: ✗ Diverges — spec §7.2 N16 and §6.1 S5 specify engine writes derivable flags to wa_term_phase2_flags; run_flag_engine() in code only writes to wa_data_quality_flags; no INSERT INTO wa_term_phase2_flags exists in engine/ code; rows written only by manual DB patch imports for pre-v9 terms

fields.spec_rule: FK to phase2_flag_types.id. Part of composite PK. Spec: derivable flags written by engine. Code: never written by engine.
fields.data_source: NOT_WRITTEN by engine — rows written only via manual DB patch imports
fields.py_file: engine/flag_engine.py (listed in docstring but not implemented)

NEW_WORD.condition: Same divergence as term_inv_id — no INSERT to wa_term_phase2_flags

GAP_FILL.condition: Same divergence as term_inv_id — no INSERT to wa_term_phase2_flags

fields.spec_match: ✗ Diverges — same as term_inv_id; the entire table is not written by v9 engine despite spec §7.2 N16 and §6.1 S5 intent
